stemHtml:
Refer to figure.
An aircraft is flying a 195 NM leg with a TAS of 120 knots. The pilot wishes to choose a cruising level to optimise fuel consumption. At 2000 ft, the aircraft has a manifold pressure of 18' and encounters a 20-knot headwind. At 4000 ft, the aircraft requires a manifold pressure of 22' and experiences a 10-knot tailwind. What is the change in fuel consumption when flying at 4000 ft compared to 2000 ft?
